title: Hybrid silicone and acrylic adhesive cover for use with wound treatment
abstract: Sealing members and methods of manufacturing the same are described. A first film layer and a second film layer each having a first side and a second side can be provided. A first adhesive can be coupled to the second side of the first film layer to form a first adhesive layer. A second adhesive can be coupled to the second side of the second film layer to form a second adhesive layer, and a third adhesive can be coupled to the first side of the second film layer to form a third adhesive layer. One or more perforations can be formed through the third adhesive layer, the second film layer, and the second adhesive layer. A first side of the third adhesive layer can be coupled to a second side of the first adhesive layer.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of manufacturing a sealing member for a dressing of a negative-pressure therapy system, the method comprising: providing a first film layer having a first side and a second side; coupling a first adhesive to the second side of the first film layer to form a first adhesive layer having a first side and a second side, the first side of the first adhesive layer coupled to the second side of the first film layer; providing a second film layer having a first side and a second side; coupling a second adhesive to the second side of the second film layer to form a second adhesive layer having a first side and a second side, the first side of the second adhesive layer coupled to the second side of the second film layer; forming one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer; and coupling the first side of the second film layer to the second side of the first adhesive layer. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein coupling the first adhesive to the second side of the first film layer comprises casting the first adhesive onto the first film layer. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein coupling the first adhesive to the second side of the first film layer comprises laminating the first film layer to the first adhesive. 4. The method of claims 2 or 3, wherein coupling the first adhesive to the second side of the first film layer further comprises curing the first adhesive. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein forming one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer comprises punching one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein forming one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer comprises cutting one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein forming one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer comprises melting one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein forming one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer comprises drilling one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer with a rotary tool. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein forming one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer comprises forming each perforation with an average effective diameter between about 2 mm and about 50 mm. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein forming one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer comprises forming each perforation with an average effective diameter of about 30 mm. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein forming one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer comprises forming each perforation with an average effective diameter of about 7 mm. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein forming one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer comprises forming one or more perforations so that adjacent perforations have centers separated by between about 5 mm and about 100 mm. 13. The method of claim 1, wherein forming one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer comprises forming one or more perforations so that adjacent perforations have centers separated by about 10 mm. 14. The method of claim 1, wherein forming one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer comprises removing about 50% of a surface area of the second film layer and the second adhesive layer. 15. The method of claim 1, wherein the second adhesive comprises a silicone gel. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the silicone gel is a two-part silicone system and the method further comprises: providing a first part of the two-part silicone system; providing a second part of the two-part silicone system; mixing the first part with the second part to form a two-part silicone gel; and curing the two-part silicone gel. 17. The method of claim 15, wherein the silicone gel is a one-part silicone. 18. The method of claim 1, wherein coupling the first side of the second film layer to the second side of the first adhesive layer comprises laminating the first side of the second film layer to the second side of the first adhesive layer. 19. The method of claim 1, wherein coupling the second adhesive to the second film layer is performed without use of a scrim layer. 20. The method of claim 1, wherein coupling the first adhesive to the first film layer is performed without use of a scrim layer. 21. The method of claim 1, wherein: coupling the first adhesive to the first film layer is performed without use of a scrim layer; and coupling the second adhesive to the second film layer is performed without use of a scrim layer. 22. The method of claim 1, wherein the second adhesive layer has a coating weight between about 150 gsm and 250 gsm. 23. The method of claim 1, wherein the first adhesive layer has a coating weight between about 10 gsm and about 60 gsm. 24. The method of claim 1, wherein the second adhesive further comprises a platinum catalyst. 25. The method of claim 1, wherein the second adhesive further comprises a sulfur catalyst. 26. The method of claim 1, further comprising forming a sizing line through the first film layer, the first adhesive layer, and the second film layer. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the sizing line comprises a plurality of sizing lines equidistantly spaced from each other across the sealing member. 28. The method of claim 26 or claim 27, wherein forming the sizing line comprises forming a plurality of perforations through the first film layer, the first adhesive layer, and the second film layer. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein the perforations comprise slits spaced about 25 mm to about 60 mm from adjacent slits. 30. The method of claim 27, wherein the plurality of sizing lines are between about 5 cm and about 30 cm from adjacent sizing lines. 31. The method of claim 27, wherein the plurality of sizing lines are about 18 cm from adjacent sizing lines. 32. The method of claim 1, wherein a transmittance of the sealing member is between about 70% and about 100%. 33. The method of claim 1, wherein a transmittance of the sealing member is about 90%. 34. The method of claim 1, further comprising: coupling a third adhesive to the second side of the second film layer to form a third adhesive layer having a first side and a second side, the first side of the third adhesive layer coupled to the second side of the second film layer and a second side of the third adhesive layer coupled to a first side of the second adhesive layer; and wherein forming one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer further comprises forming one or more perforations through the third adhesive layer. 35. The method of claim 1, further comprising: coupling a third adhesive to the first side of the second film layer to form a third adhesive layer having a first side and a second side, the second side of the third adhesive layer coupled to the first side of the second film layer and a first side of the third adhesive layer coupled to a second side of the first adhesive layer; and wherein forming one or more perforations through the second film layer and the second adhesive layer further comprises forming one or more perforations through the third adhesive layer. 36. The method of claim 35, wherein the third adhesive layer has a coating weight between about 15 and about 25 gsm. 38. The method of claim 35, wherein an absorbance of the sealing member is less than about 1. 39. The method of claim 35, wherein an absorbance of the sealing member is less than about 0.1. 40. The method of claim 35, wherein a color perceived through the sealing member is substantially the same as the color perceived independently of the sealing member. 42.
